channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Real Madrid will bring Mourinho back if Perez is re-elected
item.description: Real Madrid president Florentino Perez says he will bring Jose Mourinho back as manager if he wins Sunday's election.

item.title: US House passes Iran war powers resolution in rare moment of Trump backlash
item.description: Four Republicans allied with Democrats to pass bill to rein in Trump, though it is likely to face a presidential veto.

item.title: Who is Adam Hamawy? Doctor who served in Gaza is on the path to US Congress
item.description: Hamawy's victory in New Jersey likely to make him a rare US Congress member with firsthand experience in besieged Gaza.

item.title: Kuwait labels Iran attack ‘heinous aggression’
item.description: Kuwait’s defence ministry has labelled an attack on the country’s international airport as ‘heinous Iranian aggression’.

item.title: Ukraine attacks St Petersburg as Russia hosts its own ‘Davos’
item.description: Ukraine launched a series of air strikes targeting an oil facility and a naval air base near St Petersburg late Tuesday.

item.title: Deadly fire rips through New Delhi hotel
item.description: A fire has ripped through a hotel in New Delhi, killing at least 21 people including 18 foreign nationals.

item.title: Japan’s football team sports cowboy hats ahead of World Cup
item.description: Japan's national football team wore cowboy hats as fans welcomed them to Monterrey, Mexico.

item.title: Does Israel have nukes? ‘Most of the world assesses they do,’ says Rubio
item.description: US secretary of state dodges question at congressional hearing on Washington's position on Israel's nuclear programme.

item.title: Ten years on, world remembers Muhammad Ali ‘the Greatest’
item.description: Ten years on, world remembers Muhammad Ali 'the Greatest'

item.title: Israel’s decades-long history of invading Lebanon
item.description: Israel has invaded Lebanon for the sixth time in 48 years. Al Jazeera’s Hala Al Shami outlines this history.

item.title: Israeli strikes near Tyre hospital put youngest patient at risk
item.description: Jabal Aamel Hospital in Tyre was heavily damaged after an Israeli strike levelled nearby buildings, leaving key medical

item.title: Dalai Lama receives Grammy award for spoken-word album
item.description: Tibetan spiritual leader Tenzin Gyatso received his first Grammy award for his spoken-word album.

item.title: Gunfire erupts in Mogadishu before protests against Somali president’s rule
item.description: Ex-Somali PM Khaire accuses government forces of attacking him before planned antigovernment protests in Mogadishu.

item.title: Iran faces a new energy imbalance, but its options are limited
item.description: Iran’s government weighs limited energy control options in a strained economy, with the war impacting production.

item.title: Israeli attacks kill nine in Lebanon, reach Beirut outskirts
item.description: The attacks test a US-mediated deal to curb Israel-Hezbollah attacks, and new Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington.

item.title: Lula says Brazil cannot ‘accept treatment’ after new US tariffs proposed
item.description: Brazil's president says he was surprised by the proposed 25 percent tariffs, which came amid signs relations improving.

item.title: US cites forced labour concerns as grounds for new tariffs
item.description: USTR's proposal comes from a Section 301 unfair trade practices investigation designed to help rebuild Trump's tariffs.

item.title: Netanyahu downplays US-Israel rift after Trump confirms criticism
item.description: US president acknowledges he had harsh words for Netanyahu over Lebanon attacks but says he 'likes' the Israeli leader.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Can Asian champions Qatar reach the knockouts?
item.description: Al Jazeera speaks to Qatar coach Julen Lopetegui ahead of the World Cup, with a shot at knockouts within their grasp.

item.title: ‘Disgraceful’: Egypt jails activist Douma after article on its prisons
item.description: Rights group PEN America says Douma's 'case embodies part of an escalating crackdown on writers in Egypt'.

item.title: Bangladesh’s top diplomat elected UN General Assembly leader in tight race
item.description: Khalilur Rahman elected as the president of the UNGA's 81st session, garnering 99 votes in secret ballot.

item.title: Israeli settlers are driving Palestinian shepherds from their grazing lands
item.description: Settler attacks in the Jordan Valley have forced growing numbers of Palestinian communities to flee their villages.

item.title: What is the St Petersburg forum, Putin’s economic outreach to the world?
item.description: A Ukrainian drone strike targeted energy facilities near St Petersburg hours before the forum began.

item.title: The Ebola outbreak the world isn’t paying attention to
item.description: A deadly Ebola outbreak in the DRC is spreading across borders, with no approved vaccine or treatment for this strain.

item.title: Which World Cup 2026 stadiums will host matches in US, Canada and Mexico?
item.description: A quick look at the 16 venues across 16 cities in three countries, which will host the biggest-ever FIFA World Cup.
